advertised_host.description: Address advertised to agents during discovery; auto-detected if empty.

agent_port.description: TCP port on which the orchestrator accepts agent connections.

api_host.description: Interface address to bind the management API.

api_port.description: HTTP port on which the management API is served.

bootstrap_key.description:
Hex-encoded Ed25519 public key for the bootstrap operator.

Required on first run when no operators exist. Ignored on subsequent starts once a bootstrap operator is persisted.

bootstrap_username.description:
Human-readable username for the bootstrap operator.

Required on first run alongside [`bootstrap_key`]. Ignored on subsequent starts. Must satisfy [`ordo_store::validate_identifier`]: 1-64 Unicode letters/digits/`-`/`_`, starting with a letter.

[`bootstrap_key`]: OrchestratorConfig::bootstrap_key

drift.description: Drift detection scheduling and retention configuration.

pending_capacity.description: Maximum number of simultaneously pending agents awaiting approval.

session_token_expiry_secs.description: How many seconds a session token remains valid before expiring.

tls_cert.description:
Path to a PEM-encoded TLS certificate file.

When set alongside [`tls_key`], overrides the auto-generated self-signed certificate. Must be provided together with [`tls_key`] or startup fails.

[`tls_key`]: OrchestratorConfig::tls_key

tls_enabled.description:
Whether TLS is enabled for the management API. Defaults to `true`.

When `false`, the management API is served over plaintext HTTP and any `tls_cert` / `tls_key` values are ignored (with a warning on startup). See design `0016-encrypted-http-api.md`.

tls_key.description:
Path to a PEM-encoded TLS private key file.

Required when [`tls_cert`] is set.

[`tls_cert`]: OrchestratorConfig::tls_cert

trust_proxy_tls.description:
Whether a TLS-terminating reverse proxy fronts the management API. Defaults to `false`.

When [`tls_enabled`] is `false` (the `--no-tls` opt-out), the orchestrator normally rejects secret create and update requests so plaintext secret values never traverse an unencrypted connection (see design `0014-secrets.md`). Setting this to `true` asserts that a reverse proxy terminates TLS in front of the orchestrator, so the operator-facing hop is encrypted even though the orchestrator listens over plaintext HTTP. It permits secret writes under `--no-tls`. It has no effect when [`tls_enabled`] is `true`.

[`tls_enabled`]: OrchestratorConfig::tls_enabled

AuditConfig.description:
Configuration for the orchestrator's audit-log retention behaviour.

`retention_days` is the *configuration-file* default. The currently active retention is read from the operational store at runtime; if no operator override has been set, the orchestrator seeds the stored value from this config on startup. A value of `0` (or omitting the field entirely) means "no automatic pruning" — events accumulate until pruned manually.

retention_days.description: Default retention period in days, or `0` to disable automatic pruning. Used as the seed value when the operational store has no override.

DriftConfig.description: Configuration for drift detection scheduling and retention.

enabled.description: Whether periodic drift checking is enabled. Defaults to `true`.

interval.description: Seconds between drift checks per agent. Defaults to 3600 (1 hour).

max_concurrent.description: Maximum number of in-flight drift checks across all agents. Defaults to 8.

retention_days.description: How long to retain drift records, in days. Defaults to 30.

skip_recently_applied.description: Suppress periodic drift checks for this many seconds after an apply completes. Defaults to 300 (5 minutes).

MetricsConfig.description: Configuration for the orchestrator's metrics collection behaviour.

poll_interval.description: How often the orchestrator polls connected agents for metrics, in seconds.

retention.description: How long to retain metrics samples, in seconds.
